headline: Certificate Transparency log-list signature verification arrives as pki.ct.verifyLogListSignature: verify the detached signature published alongside the CT log list against a caller-pinned signer key, completing the offline log-list trust chain.
summary: The Certificate Transparency log list is published with a detached signature (log_list.sig over log_list.json) so a relying party can confirm the list is the authentic one the operator signed before trusting the logs in it. pki.ct.verifyLogListSignature(json, signature, publicKey) verifies that signature: json is the raw log-list bytes (verified byte-for-byte, never re-serialized), signature is the detached signature, and publicKey is the caller-pinned signer SubjectPublicKeyInfo -- there is no baked-in key, matching the offline, pin-it-yourself stance of the rest of the toolkit. The scheme is RSASSA-PKCS1-v1.5 with SHA-256 over an RSA key (the deployed scheme), with an EC P-256 / ECDSA-SHA-256 arm for future-proofing. It resolves true for a valid signature and false on a cryptographic mismatch (a verdict), and fails closed before any verification on a forgeable key: an RSA public exponent below 3, a sub-2048-bit RSA key, an unsupported key type or curve, or a non-conformant ECDSA signature each throw a typed CtError. Together with pki.ct.parseLogList, this closes the offline log-list trust chain: verify the list's signature against your pinned key, then ingest it. Cross-checked against OpenSSL.

Added: pki.ct.verifyLogListSignature(json, signature, publicKey) verifies the detached signature over the Certificate Transparency log list (log_list.sig over log_list.json). The message is the raw json bytes, verified byte-for-byte with no canonicalization; publicKey is the caller-pinned signer SubjectPublicKeyInfo (there is no embedded key). The scheme is RSASSA-PKCS1-v1.5 with SHA-256 (an EC P-256 arm is accepted for future-proofing). It resolves true or false (a cryptographic verdict) and throws a typed CtError on a forgeable or unsupported key (an RSA exponent below 3, a sub-2048-bit RSA key, an unsupported key type or curve, a non-conformant ECDSA DER signature). Its verdict is cross-checked against OpenSSL's dgst -verify. Paired with pki.ct.parseLogList, this completes the offline CT log-list trust chain.
